channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇨🇺 US Justice Department Prepares to Indict Former Cuban President Raul Castro
item.description: The US Justice Department is working to secure criminal charges against former Cuban President Raul Castro related to the 1996 shootdown of humanitarian planes, amid heightened US-Cuba tensions.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israel and Lebanon Agree to Extend Ceasefire by 45 Days After Washington Talks
item.description: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend their US-brokered ceasefire for 45 days to allow for more negotiations, despite continued flare-ups between Israel and Hezbollah.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Trump Suggests Deadly Russian Strike on Kyiv Could Set Back Ukraine Peace Efforts
item.description: President Donald Trump suggested that a Russian missile strike on a Kyiv apartment building that killed 24 people, including three children, could complicate efforts to find a peaceful settlement to the war in Ukraine.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelenskiy Vows Retribution After Russian Strike Kills 24 in Kyiv Apartment Building
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy promised retribution against Russia after a Russian missile strike on a Kyiv apartment building killed 24 people, including three children.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Trump Weighs Taiwan Arms Package After Summit Aimed at Steadying US-China Ties
item.description: President Donald Trump said he has not decided whether to proceed with a major arms sale to Taiwan, adding to uncertainty about US support for the island following his summit with Xi Jinping in Beijing.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Doubles Down on Manufacturing Push After Trump Visit
item.description: China's Communist Party journal calls for strengthening manufacturing shortly after Trump's departure from Beijing, signaling continued focus on industrial self-sufficiency amid trade tensions.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Faces Critical Shortage of Accredited Language Schools for Foreign Workers
item.description: Only three schools nationwide are certified to teach under Japan's new foreign worker training program, creating a bottleneck as the country seeks to address labor shortages through immigration.

item.title: 🛡️ US Army Trains to Counter Drone Swarms as Battlefield Threat Evolves
item.description: The US Army is developing tactics to combat multiple drone threats simultaneously, moving beyond individual counter-drone measures as adversaries increasingly deploy swarm tactics.

item.title: 🕵️ FBI Offers $200K Reward for Ex-Air Force Specialist Who Defected to Iran
item.description: Monica Witt, a former US Air Force counterintelligence specialist, is wanted for espionage after allegedly defecting to Iran and sharing classified information about US intelligence personnel.

item.title: 🇺🇳 UN Vehicle Struck Twice by Drones in Ukraine's Kherson Region
item.description: The UN expressed alarm after a humanitarian vehicle was hit twice by drones in Kherson, calling it a serious incident. The organization is working to establish the facts and has not attributed the attack to either side.

item.title: 🇨🇳✈️ China Agrees to Buy 200 Boeing Jets, Could Rise to 750
item.description: President Trump announced China will purchase 200 Boeing aircraft with GE engines, with potential to expand to 750 jets. The deal emerged from the Beijing summit between Trump and Xi Jinping.

item.title: 🇩🇪🇺🇸 German Chancellor Merz Reports Good Call With Trump After Iran Spat
item.description: Friedrich Merz initiated a phone call with Trump following tensions over Iran policy. Merz, who previously expressed admiration for America, sought to smooth relations after criticizing US actions.

item.title: 🌾💰 US Expects Double-Digit Billions in Chinese Farm Purchases After Summit
item.description: USTR Jamieson Greer announced China will commit to massive agricultural purchases following the Trump-Xi summit. The deal aims to reduce trade imbalance between the two nations.

item.title: ⛏️🇨🇳 China Still Slow on Rare Earth Approvals Despite Improvements
item.description: USTR Greer noted that while rare earth exports to the US are improving, Beijing continues to delay some shipments. Rare earths remain a key point of leverage in US-China trade negotiations.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Trump Leaves Beijing With Few Wins But Warm Words for Xi
item.description: President Trump departed China after two days of talks without major breakthroughs on trade or Iran, but with praise for Xi Jinping. Key issues like Taiwan and AI remain unresolved.

item.title: 🇺🇬 Uganda Confirms Ebola Outbreak
item.description: Uganda health ministry announced an Ebola outbreak involving the Bundibugyo strain, as neighboring DRC battles a separate outbreak that has killed 65. The dual outbreaks raise regional containment concerns.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US CDC Monitoring Ebola Outbreaks in DRC and Uganda
item.description: The US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention is providing technical assistance and monitoring the Ebola outbreaks in both Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, its acting director said Friday.

item.title: 🦠 New Ebola outbreak confirmed in Congo with 65 deaths
item.description: A new Ebola outbreak has been confirmed in Congo's Ituri province with 246 suspected cases and 65 deaths. The outbreak comes five months after Congo's previous outbreak was declared over, raising fears of wider spread near Uganda and South Sudan borders.

item.title: 🇧🇾 Russia planning new offensive from Belarus, Zelensky warns
item.description: President Zelensky said Russia is stepping up efforts to drag Belarus into the war, with Moscow planning potential attacks on Ukraine or NATO states from Belarusian territory. Ukraine has strengthened its northern border defenses in response.

item.title: 🛸 Russian drone force passes 100,000 personnel, copying Ukraine model
item.description: Russia has expanded its drone forces from 86,000 to over 114,000 personnel and is on track to reach 168,000 by year-end, effectively copying Ukraine's Unmanned Systems Forces structure, according to Ukrainian commander Robert "Magyar" Brovdi.

item.title: 🇦🇪 UAE rejects Iranian accusations of war involvement
item.description: The UAE Foreign Ministry rejected Iranian claims that Abu Dhabi was an "active partner" in the US-Israel war, calling them attempts to justify Iranian attacks on the Gulf nation. Iran had accused the UAE of involvement after the BRICS meeting in New Delhi.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇹🇼 Trump Discusses Taiwan Arms Sales With Xi Jinping
item.description: President Trump revealed he discussed US arms sales to Taiwan during his Beijing summit with Xi Jinping, saying a decision would come soon. The issue remains a major flashpoint in US-China relations.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Trump Says Patience With Iran Running Thin
item.description: President Trump stated he would not be "much more patient" with Iran as negotiations continue, while suggesting the quest to retrieve enriched uranium was more about perception than security.

item.title: 🇨🇳🛢️ Trump Discussed Lifting Sanctions on Chinese Companies Buying Iranian Oil
item.description: President Trump revealed he discussed potentially lifting sanctions on Chinese companies purchasing Iranian oil during his Beijing visit, with a decision expected soon.

item.title: 🇨🇺🇺🇸 CIA Director Ratcliffe Meets Cuban Officials in Havana
item.description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe held high-level talks with Cuban officials including Raúl Castros grandson, discussing intelligence cooperation and security issues amid tensions over US energy blockade.
